title: Executive Order 9587—Amending Executive Order 8588 Entitled "Prescribing Regulations Governing the Payment of Expenses of Transportation of Household Goods and Personal Effects of Certain Civilian Officers and Employees of the United States" and Prescribing Regulations Governing the Payment of Expenses of Transportation of the Immediate Family of Civilian Officers and Employees of the Government

flags.justification: A narrow, technical benefits-regulation amendment with specific weight and time limits.
Precise, specific criteria throughout (exact weight limits, exact time windows, defined extension conditions).
Related transportation-benefit provisions all coherently amend one existing regulatory framework.
A benefits-eligibility regulation, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A standing benefits regulation; the wartime-specific transportation-means provision is itself explicitly time-limited to the war plus six months.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
An extremely narrow, technical benefits-regulation amendment.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
